analysis.consistency: High. The feature clearly defines when and how Eldritch Invocations are gained (at 2nd level and subsequent levels) and allows for swapping known invocations upon leveling up, which aligns with standard D&D 5e Warlock mechanics.
analysis.quality: Good. The description is clear and directly addresses the core mechanic of the Warlock's signature customization feature. The cross-reference to the Warlock table (though not present in the raw data) is standard practice for this feature.

mechanics.progression: More invocations gained at specific Warlock levels (as per Warlock table).
mechanics.modification: Can swap one known invocation for a different one when gaining a new Warlock level, provided the new choice meets the requirements for the current level.
mechanics.notes: This feature grants access to a pool of permanent, modular magical abilities that define the Warlock's specialization.
